title: Fix Roadmap Enter-to-jump (handle jumpToEpicMsg) + dead-seam nits
description:
Final review of epic 682 found two wired-but-dead paths. Fix:
1. app.go Update has NO case for jumpToEpicMsg (emitted by view_roadmap.go on Enter), so Roadmap Enter-to-jump is a silent no-op. Add a case that sets the sidebar scope to that epic node (mirror the old jumpToRoadmapEpic in model.go) and switches the active view to List, so the jump is visible. Add a test.
2. Palette jump (paletteJumpMsg) only works when the List view is active (JumpTo is implemented only on listView). Make a jump from any view switch to List first, then select the target. (Minimal: in the paletteJumpMsg handler, set active view to List before JumpTo.)
3. Remove the dead paletteOpenMsg seam (palette.go ~line 17 — defined, never emitted/handled).
4. Fix the stale comment in view_timeline_test.go (~line 351) that says Timeline=hotkey 3; it is 4 (List=1, Board=2, Roadmap=3, Timeline=4).

Do NOT touch the model.go dead-code cleanup (separate follow-up tick). Files: internal/tui/app.go, internal/tui/palette.go, internal/tui/view_timeline_test.go, + a test. Reuse harness helpers. If a shared golden drifts, regenerate only the ones your change affects.
acceptance_criteria: go test -short ./internal/tui/... green; new test: Enter on a Roadmap-view epic sets scope to that epic and switches to List (the selected tick/scope reflects the jump); palette jump works when a non-List view is active (switches to List then selects); dead paletteOpenMsg removed; stale Timeline hotkey comment corrected
closed_reason: Completed: jumpToEpicMsg handler (Roadmap Enter jumps to epic in List); palette jump switches to List first; SelectNode wrapper; removed dead paletteOpenMsg; fixed stale comment; +2 tests
